name: MEDIAN ARCUATE LIGAMENT COMPRESSION
definition: MALS = CELIAC AXIS COMPRESSION SYNDROME = DUNBAR SYNDROME dynamic compression of celiac artery (± superior mesenteric artery) by median arcuate

demographics.Age: 30-84 years; M÷F=2÷3; especially young thin women
demographics.Incidence: 50-60% stenosis in 5-12% of healthy adults during inspiration / in 16% during

dominant_finding: peak systolic velocity in celiac artery ≥ 250 cm/s (70-99% stenosis) or in SMA ≥ 275
distribution: focal narrowing in proximal celiac axis (more prominent in expiration → superior
discriminating_features: superior indentation of proximal celiac a. creating a CHARACTERISTIC "hooked" appearance
focal narrowing in proximal celiac axis (more prominent in expiration → superior
direct visualization of median arcuate ligament
collateral filling via SMA + pancreaticoduodenal arcades

general: focal narrowing in proximal celiac axis (more prominent in expiration → superior
superior indentation of proximal celiac a. creating a CHARACTERISTIC "hooked" appearance
direct visualization of median arcuate ligament

absence of atherosclerotic changes in adjacent aorta and proximal celiac segment
US: peak systolic velocity in celiac artery ≥ 250 cm/s (70-99% stenosis) or in SMA ≥ 275
normalization of elevated velocity during deep inspiration / standing upright position

clinical.Rx: surgical division of median arcuate lig. (50% long-term cure) ± celiac artery stenting
